description: Targets the new Plan-5 rules (Acyclic-Dependencies, cognitive-complexity, BFLA, BOPLA, mock-echo). Every seed sits on a '+' line of diff.txt; every trap is genuinely out of scope per the agents' Do-NOT-Flag lists (size-only growth, clear-non-duplicative shape, framework-escaped input).

seeds.why: Introduces a dependency CYCLE: src/services/orders.ts already imports chargeOrder from ./billing (pre-existing context), and this '+' line makes billing import back from ./orders. The diff-introduced edge billing->orders closes the cycle. CLAUDE.md requires the service graph stay acyclic; break it by hoisting the shared total logic into a third module.

classifyOrder went from a single ternary to deeply nested conditionals (status branch -> total branch -> backordered branch -> priority ternary), materially raising cognitive complexity with little raw growth. Flag the nested-branching spike (the deepest nesting line is the clearest cite); suggest a flat lookup / early-return decomposition. This targets the cognitive-complexity rule, distinct from raw size.

cancelAllOrders is an admin-only action (cancels every order in the tenant via updateMany({})) but performs only the authenticated-session check, NO function-level authorization. Any authenticated principal can invoke it. Add the canonical privilege check (if (!session.isAdmin) return forbidden(res)) before the write.

updateOrder spreads the entire request body into the $set (mass-assignment). Even though the filter is ownership-scoped, the spread lets a client write fields it should not control (e.g. ownerId, status, privilege flags). Replace with an explicit allowlist of updatable fields per CLAUDE.md.

The getOrderTotal test mocks db.orders.findOne to resolve { total: 42 } and then asserts the result is 42 — it asserts only the value its own mock was configured to return. The test exercises no real logic and would pass regardless of getOrderTotal's behavior (mock-echo). Assert on transformed/derived behavior or drop the mock.

traps.whyNotFlagged: size-only growth, no second symptom — summarizeOrder got longer but stays a flat, single-concern list-builder with no nesting, no mixed concerns, no coupling. The Plan-5 architecture size rule needs a SECOND symptom; pure length growth alone must not be flagged.

clear-non-duplicative shape — statusLabel is a small explicit mapper that is clear and not duplicative of anything. Architecture's Do-NOT-Flag list says do not raise 'could be more abstract' when the current shape is clear and non-duplicative (concrete > generic). Flagging it as premature/under-abstracted is a regression.

traps.lineHint: const rows = await db.query("SELECT * FROM orders WHERE owner = ? AND note LIKE ?", [session.userId, req.query.q]);
traps.whyNotFlagged: framework-escaped input — req.query.q is passed as a bound ? parameter; the data layer escapes bound values by default (CLAUDE.md). Security's Do-NOT-Flag list excludes theoretical injection where the framework already escapes. Flagging this as SQL injection is a regression.
